title: Collapsible markdown component with heading-based sections
purpose: Renders markdown content with collapsible sections based on heading levels, allowing users to expand/collapse content blocks. Supports syntax highlighting, interactive checkboxes, and theme switching.
module_context: Exports a CollapsibleMarkdown component that parses markdown into hierarchical sections, manages collapsible state, and renders content with custom styling and interactive elements.

methods.name: parseMarkdownSections
methods.normalized_params: (markdown: string): { preamble: string; preambleStartLine: number; sections: Section[] }

steps.content: Split the markdown content into individual lines.
Process each line to identify headings and track content between them.
Track fenced code blocks to avoid treating their content as headings.
For each heading, create a new section and add it to the appropriate parent section.
Build a hierarchical tree of sections based on heading levels.
Return the preamble content and parsed sections with their metadata.
Check if the content is empty and return null if so.
Memoize the section-specific components with adjusted checkbox handlers.
Render the markdown content using ReactMarkdown with configured plugins and components.
Render a collapsible section with the given title and level.
Render the content of the section using the markdown content component.
Recursively render any child sections of this section.
Parse the markdown content into sections and preamble using the parser function.
Define base components for rendering markdown elements with appropriate styling.
Merge extra components provided by the caller with the base components.
Count the total number of sections including nested ones.
Render the controls for managing collapsible sections if there are any.
Render the preamble content before the first heading.
Render each top-level section using the section renderer component.
Join the accumulated content lines into a single string.
Count leading blank lines that will be stripped by trim.
Trim the content to remove leading and trailing whitespace.
Adjust the starting line number to account for leading blank lines.
Assign the content and adjusted start line to either the current section or preamble.
Reset the content accumulator for the next section.
Recursively count the number of sections in the hierarchy.
For each section, add one to the count and recursively count its children.
